<!-- docgen:version=0.1.0 reason: first generation of this directory's README -->

## Purpose
This is the `secret-scrub` subsystem — it detects and redacts secrets (Solana keypairs, BIP39 mnemonics, GitHub/OpenAI tokens, PEM private keys, `*_SECRET=` env assignments, tokenized git remote URLs) across four leak pathways: staged commits, Claude session transcripts, uncommitted working-tree files, and `.git/config`. It owns both the detection engine and the installers that wire it into pre-commit hooks and OS schedulers.

## Files
- `detect.py` — pure, side-effect-free detection engine; `find_secrets(text, filename) -> list[Finding]` returns sorted, de-overlapped findings (specific kinds beat generic `env_secret` on overlap), and `is_whole_file_secret(text, filename)` flags files (`.env`, `*.pem`, `*keypair*.json`, id_*) that should be unstaged rather than redacted. Honors inline `# secret-scrub: allow` escape hatch per line.
- `scrub.py` — the CLI entry point and only stateful module; dispatches `--staged` (pre-commit redact/unstage, exit 1 = secret remains), `--sessions` (incremental redact of `~/.claude/projects/*.jsonl` via mtime state file), `--working-trees <path>...` (scan modified+untracked files), and `--git-configs <path>... [--fix]` (strip tokens from remote URLs). Owns `scrub_staged`, `scrub_sessions`, `scrub_working_trees`, and redaction/state helpers.
- `install.sh` — installs the pre-commit hook into one target repo by writing a shim that calls `scrub.py` by ABSOLUTE path; handles Case A (delegating to `pre-commit-local` under a global `core.hooksPath`) vs Case B (plain `.git/hooks/pre-commit`). Never touches global git config.
- `install-launchd.sh` — macOS: installs/reloads the `com.secret-scrub` launchd job running `scrub.py --sessions` on `StartInterval` (default 1800s). `install`/`uninstall`/`status`; honors `DRY_RUN=1`.
- `install-launchd-trees.sh` — macOS: installs the `com.secret-scrub-trees` launchd job that runs `scrub-working-trees-runner.sh`; seeds an empty `~/.config/secret-scrub/working-trees.txt` config on first install.
- `install-systemd.sh` — Linux: systemd `--user` timer+service equivalent of `install-launchd.sh` (sessions scan); `enable --now`, `Persistent=true` catches up missed runs.
- `install-systemd-trees.sh` — Linux: systemd `--user` timer+service equivalent of `install-launchd-trees.sh` (working-trees scan); reads the same config file.
- `scrub-working-trees-runner.sh` — bash 3.2-compatible wrapper invoked by both schedulers; reads repo paths from the config file (skips comments/blanks, manually expands leading `~`), then `exec`s `scrub.py --working-trees`.
- `test_detect.py` — unit tests for `detect.py`: each secret kind, false-positive rejection (non-byte arrays, prose), allow-comment suppression, whole-file detection.
- `test_scrub.py` — integration tests in hermetic temp repos: staged redaction/unstaging, private-key rotate warning, `--sessions` redaction, and `--git-configs` token detection/`--fix`.
- `test_install.py` — tests `install.sh` in temp git repos covering Case A, Case B, uninstall, and idempotency.

## Subdirectories
- `githooks/` — holds the committed `pre-commit` shim that runs the scrubber against staged files at commit time. See `secret-scrub/githooks/README.md` for detail.

## Dependencies / collaborators
- `detect.py` ← `scrub.py` imports `find_secrets` / `is_whole_file_secret` via `sys.path` insert of its own dir; the two are tightly coupled — a new detector pattern in `detect.py` warrants a `full=True` re-scan in `scrub.py`.
- The installed pre-commit hook calls `scrub.py` by absolute path, so the guarded repo needs nothing vendored — but moving/renaming this directory breaks every already-installed hook.
- `~/.config/secret-scrub/working-trees.txt` — user-edited config shared by both `*-trees` installers and the runner; empty by default, so the working-trees scan is a no-op until populated.

## Gotchas
- **The pre-commit path fails OPEN.** Only `scrub.py --staged` exit code `1` blocks a commit; missing python3/scrub.py or any other exit code lets the commit through. A green commit does not prove the scan ran. (Note: the child `githooks/pre-commit` hardcodes `tools/secret-scrub/scrub.py`, while `install.sh` writes an absolute path — verify which hook is actually active.)
- **`--sessions` is incremental and saves state AFTER scanning**, so files written by other processes mid-scan may not be caught until the run-after-next (worst case ~one 30-min cycle of latency). Use `full=True` to force a complete re-scan.
- **Mnemonic detection is a heuristic, not BIP39-validated** — any run of 12/24 short lowercase words false-positives; rely on the `# secret-scrub: allow` comment for those. Detection redacts the on-disk copy but never rotates an already-leaked key.
